articles.title: Using Twitter Spaces to Warm Up Your Token Launch Audience
articles.summary: A tactical playbook for using Twitter Spaces to nurture your community 2–4 weeks before token generation event (TGE).
articles.content: In this guide, we break down a 4-week Twitter Spaces calendar leading into your TGE, including topics, guest formats, and CTAs that prime your community for conversion. We cover how to repurpose Spaces recordings into short-form video, coordinate with KOLs, and track performance with UTMs across your launch funnel...

articles.title: Multi-Chain Token Launch: Video Funnel Playbook
articles.summary: How to structure a cross-chain token launch video funnel from awareness to post-TGE education.
articles.content: Launching on multiple chains introduces complexity in messaging, compliance, and creative execution. This article maps out a three-layer funnel: top-of-funnel hype trailers, mid-funnel explainers tailored to each chain’s users, and post-launch utility walkthroughs...

articles.title: Designing Airdrop Onboarding Videos for Token Launches
articles.summary: Reduce support tickets and failed claims during your token airdrop with clear, locale-aware onboarding videos.
articles.content: Airdrop campaigns can frustrate users when wallet flows and claim conditions aren’t crystal clear. In this article we outline a three-part video structure: pre-airdrop announcement, claim walkthrough, and security reminders...

articles.title: Improving Whitelist Conversion with Pre-Mint Video Sequences
articles.summary: Turn passive whitelist signups into active minters with a structured video nurturing sequence.
articles.content: Most teams treat whitelist as a vanity metric. We show how to segment your list by intent, trigger tailored explainer videos, and bake urgency into your content schedule leading into mint and TGE...

articles.title: Explaining Liquidity and Emissions in Your Token Launch Videos
articles.summary: How to communicate liquidity bootstrapping, emissions, and vesting without scaring away non-technical users.
articles.content: Token economics are often the hardest part of a launch to message. We share visual frameworks and analogies that keep compliance teams happy while still being digestible for retail users...

articles.title: Storyboarding DeFi Yield Explainers for Non-Crypto Natives
articles.summary: A visual approach to explaining staking, lending, and LP positions to first-time DeFi users.
articles.content: We outline a storyboard structure for yield explainers: problem framing, base concept, risk framing, and next steps...

articles.title: Do’s and Don’ts of NFT Collection Trailers
articles.summary: Common pitfalls and best practices when promoting PFP and art collections with trailers.
articles.content: From over-promising roadmaps to unclear mint details, we break down patterns from 200+ NFT trailers...

articles.title: Talking About Smart Contract Security in Your Launch Promos
articles.summary: How far you can go when referencing audits, formal verification, and bug bounties in public-facing content.
articles.content: Security communication is a tightrope between marketing clarity and legal nuance. We explain how to position audits, what disclaimers to use, and how to avoid implying guarantees...

articles.title: KOL Disclosures and Disclaimers in Web3 Video
articles.summary: Regulatory and platform-specific disclosure requirements when using creators and KOLs.
articles.content: We cover FTC guidance, regional differences, and how to integrate disclosures into video without killing watch time...

articles.title: Inside Our Web3 Video Production Pipeline
articles.summary: From discovery to final delivery, how we run production for DeFi, NFT, and metaverse clients.
articles.content: We walk through our 7-phase pipeline, tools, and typical timelines for 30–90 second pieces...

articles.title: Measuring Performance of Web3 Video Campaigns
articles.summary: Beyond basic view counts: KPIs that matter for token launches and protocol videos.
articles.content: We detail retention curves, assisted conversions, and community metrics that tie video performance to business results...

articles.title: Concepting Teasers for Metaverse Event Experiences
articles.summary: Unpublished draft exploring concepts for metaverse event teasers.

faq_items.question: How do you handle smart contract security in promos?
faq_items.answer: We never make absolute security guarantees or imply that a contract is "unhackable." Instead, we reference security measures factually: audits (with auditor names and dates), formal verification where applicable, and active bug bounty programs. Our legal checklist ensures all on-screen claims are backed by documentation you provide, and we add on-screen disclaimers where required (e.g., "No investment advice" and "Smart contracts may contain risks"). For highly regulated jurisdictions, we coordinate final copy with your counsel before locking scripts.

faq_items.question: Can you mention our smart contract audits and bug bounties in the video?
faq_items.answer: Yes, as long as you provide verifiable documentation. We can reference completed audits, auditors, and high-level findings without overstating their scope. We avoid phrases like "fully secure" and instead use language such as "independently audited by [firm]" or "ongoing bug bounty program on [platform]."

faq_items.question: Do you localize disclaimers for different regions during token launches?
faq_items.answer: We support regionalized disclaimer slates and subtitles for major jurisdictions (US, EU, UK, and selected APAC regions). You or your counsel provide jurisdiction-specific language; we then build versions of the video with matching legal copy and on-screen text.

faq_items.question: How long does it take to produce a 60-second explainer?
faq_items.answer: Most 60-second explainers take 4–6 weeks from brief to final delivery, depending on complexity and your review speed. Our client portal shows milestone dates and lets you request timeline adjustments.

faq_items.question: Do you offer installment plans for metaverse packages?
faq_items.answer: Yes. Selected metaverse packages can be split into 3–6 monthly installments. The Services page will display a "View Monthly Payment Option" toggle where installments are available.

faq_items.question: How do you handle sensitive protocol data in DeFi explainers?
faq_items.answer: We work only with anonymized or publicly available data unless otherwise agreed in writing. Access to any private dashboards or staging environments is restricted to your assigned production team and covered by NDA.

faq_items.question: Can we use holders’ NFTs inside the promo video?
faq_items.answer: In most cases, yes, but you must confirm your collection’s licensing terms. For CC0 and commercial-rights collections, we can feature certain tokens; for more restrictive licenses, we may need explicit permission from holders.

faq_items.question: How do you capture footage inside metaverse platforms?
faq_items.answer: We combine in-world screen capture, camera tools (where supported), and compositing with motion graphics. For some platforms we also use custom shaders and dev tools provided by your team.

faq_items.question: How many revision rounds are included?
faq_items.answer: Most standard packages include 2–3 revision rounds per major phase (script, storyboard, animation). Details are listed on each Service Package page.

faq_items.question: Can we view pricing in both USD and EUR?
faq_items.answer: Yes. Use the currency selector in the site header to toggle between USD and EUR. Package prices and monthly installments will update automatically.
